prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on ethylene (C₂H₄, 6 atoms) performed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', density-fit RKS) in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken as-is from the user-supplied XYZ file (single-point; no re-optimization at this level). SCF converged in 6 cycles. All integrity checks passed; result flagged trustworthy. No warnings reported.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV (HOMO = −7.6155818 eV, LUMO = −0.17986527 eV). These are Kohn–Sham orbital eigenvalue differences (Koopmans-style), not true vertical excitation energies. Total electronic energy = −78.623486 Hartree (B3LYP/def2-TZVP). Absolute energies are defined relative to the bare-nuclei + free-electron origin of this method and must not be compared across different methods or basis sets.
